links.title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning

papers.summary: We introduce Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning (HACRL), a new learning paradigm that addresses the inefficiencies of isolated on-policy optimization. HACRL enables collaborative optimization with independent execution: heterogeneous agents share verified rollouts during training to mutually improve, while operating independently at inference time. Unlike LLM-based multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), HACRL does not require coordinated deployment, and unlike on-/off-policy distillation, it enables bidirectional mutual learning among heterogeneous agents rather than one-directional teacher-to-student transfer. Building on this paradigm, we propose HACPO, a collaborative RL algorithm that enables principled rollout sharing to maximize sample utilization and cross-agent knowledge transfer. To mitigate capability discrepancies and policy distribution shifts, HACPO introduces four tailored mechanisms with theoretical guarantees on unbiased advantage estimation and optimization correctness. Extensive experiments across diverse heterogeneous model combinations and reasoning benchmarks show that HACPO consistently improves all participating agents, outperforming GSPO by an average of 3.3\% while using only half the rollout cost.
papers.summary_zh: 我們引入了 Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning (HACRL)，這是一種新的學習範式，解決了孤立 on-policy optimization 的低效率問題。HACRL 實現了協同優化與獨立執行：異質代理在訓練期間共享經過驗證的 rollouts 以相互改進，同時在 inference time 獨立運行。與基於 LLM 的 multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) 不同，HACRL 不需要協調部署；與 on-/off-policy distillation 不同，它實現了異質代理之間的雙向相互學習，而不是單向的師生傳遞。基於這種範式，我們提出了 HACPO，一種協作 RL 演算法，它實現了原則性的 rollout 共享，以最大化 sample utilization 和 cross-agent knowledge transfer。為了緩解 capability discrepancies 和 policy distribution shifts，HACPO 引入了四種量身定制的機制，這些機制在 unbiased advantage estimation 和 optimization correctness 方面具有理論保證。對各種異質模型組合和推理基準進行的廣泛實驗表明，HACPO 持續改進了所有參與代理，平均優於 GSPO 3.3%，同時僅使用一半的 rollout cost。
papers.title: Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning
papers.title_zh: 異質代理協作強化學習

papers.summary: Adam is the default algorithm for training neural networks, including large language models (LLMs). However, \citet{reddi2019convergence} provided an example that Adam diverges, raising concerns for its deployment in AI model training. We identify a key mismatch between the divergence example and practice: \citet{reddi2019convergence} pick the problem after picking the hyperparameters of Adam, i.e., $(β_1,β_2)$; while practical applications often fix the problem first and then tune $(β_1,β_2)$.
papers.summary_zh: Adam 是訓練 neural networks，包括 large language models (LLMs) 的預設演算法。然而，\citet{reddi2019convergence} 提供了一個 Adam 發散的例子，引發了對其在 AI 模型訓練中部署的擔憂。我們發現發散例子與實際應用之間存在一個關鍵的不匹配：\citet{reddi2019convergence} 是在選定 Adam 的超參數 (即 $(β_1,β_2)$) 之後才選擇問題；而實際應用通常是先確定問題，然後再調整 $(β_1,β_2)$。

links.title: SageBwd: A Trainable Low-bit Attention

primary_link.title: SageBwd: A Trainable Low-bit Attention

radar.summary: Low-bit attention, such as SageAttention, has emerged as an effective approach for accelerating model inference, but its applicability to training remains poorly understood. In prior work, we introduced SageBwd, a trainable INT8 attention that quantizes six of seven attention matrix multiplications while preserving fine-tuning performance. However, SageBwd exhibited a persistent performance gap to full-precision attention (FPA) during pre-training. In this work, we investigate why this gap occurs and demonstrate that SageBwd matches full-precision attention during pretraining. Through experiments and theoretical analysis, we reach a few important insights and conclusions: (i) QK-norm is necessary for stable training at large tokens per step, (ii) quantization errors primarily arise from the backward-pass score gradient dS, (iii) reducing tokens per step enables SageBwd to match FPA performance in pre-training, and (iv) K-smoothing remains essential for training stability, while Q-smoothing provides limited benefit during pre-training.
radar.summary_zh: 低位元注意力 (Low-bit attention)，例如 SageAttention，已成為加速模型推理的有效方法，但其在訓練中的適用性仍知之甚少。在先前的工作中，我們引入了 SageBwd，這是一種可訓練的 INT8 attention，它量化了七個 attention 矩陣乘法中的六個，同時保持了 fine-tuning 性能。然而，SageBwd 在 pre-training 期間與全精度 attention (FPA) 存在持續的性能差距。在這項工作中，我們探究了為何會出現這種差距，並證明 SageBwd 在 pre-training 期間可與全精度 attention 匹配。透過實驗和理論分析，我們得出了一些重要的見解和結論：(i) 在每個步驟 (per step) 大量 tokens 的情況下，QK-norm 對於穩定訓練是必要的；(ii) 量化誤差主要源於反向傳播的分數梯度 dS (backward-pass score gradient dS)；(iii) 減少每個步驟的 tokens 數 (tokens per step) 可以使 SageBwd 在 pre-training 中匹配 FPA 的性能；(iv) K-smoothing 對於訓練穩定性仍然至關重要，而 Q-smoothing 在 pre-training 期間提供的益處有限。

links.title: ParEVO: Synthesizing Code for Irregular Data: High-Performance Parallelism through Agentic Evolution

primary_link.title: ParEVO: Synthesizing Code for Irregular Data: High-Performance Parallelism through Agentic Evolution

top5.summary:
The transition from sequential to parallel computing is essential for modern high-performance applications but is hindered by the steep learning curve of concurrent programming. This challenge is magnified for irregular data structures (such as sparse graphs, unbalanced trees, and non-uniform meshes) where static scheduling fails and data dependencies are unpredictable. Current Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail catastrophically on these tasks, generating code plagued by subtle race conditions, deadlocks, and sub-optimal scaling.
  We bridge this gap with ParEVO, a framework designed to synthesize high-performance parallel algorithms for irregular data. Our contributions include: (1) The Parlay-Instruct Corpus, a curated dataset of 13,820 tasks synthesized via a "Critic-Refine" pipeline that explicitly filters for empirically performant algorithms that effectively utilize Work-Span parallel primitives; (2) specialized DeepSeek, Qwen, and Gemini models fine-tuned to align probabilistic generation with the rigorous semantics of the ParlayLib library; and (3) an Evolutionary Coding Agent (ECA) that improves the "last mile" of correctness by iteratively repairing code using feedback from compilers, dynamic race detectors, and performance profilers.
  On the ParEval benchmark, ParEVO achieves an average 106x speedup (with a maximum of 1103x) across the suite, and a robust 13.6x speedup specifically on complex irregular graph problems, outperforming state-of-the-art commercial models. Furthermore, our evolutionary approach matches state-of-the-art expert human baselines, achieving up to a 4.1x speedup on specific highly-irregular kernels. Source code and datasets are available at https://github.com/WildAlg/ParEVO.
top5.summary_zh:
從循序運算轉向平行運算是現代高效能應用程式的關鍵，但卻受制於並行程式設計陡峭的學習曲線。對於不規則資料結構（例如稀疏圖、不平衡樹和非均勻網格）來說，這項挑戰更加嚴峻，因為靜態排程會失效且資料依賴性不可預測。目前的 Large Language Models (LLMs) 在這些任務上經常遭遇災難性失敗，生成的程式碼充斥著微妙的 race conditions、deadlocks 和次優的 scaling。
我們透過 ParEVO 彌補了這一差距，這是一個旨在為不規則資料合成高效能平行演算法的框架。我們的貢獻包括：(1) Parlay-Instruct Corpus，一個透過「Critic-Refine」流程合成的策劃資料集，包含 13,820 個任務，明確篩選出能有效利用 Work-Span 平行原語的經驗上高效能演算法；(2) 經過 fine-tuned 的專門 DeepSeek、Qwen 和 Gemini 模型，旨在將機率生成與 ParlayLib 函式庫嚴格的語義對齊；以及 (3) 一個 Evolutionary Coding Agent (ECA)，它透過利用編譯器、動態 race detectors 和性能 profilers 的回饋，迭代修復程式碼，從而改善「最後一哩路」的正確性。
在 ParEval 基準測試中，ParEVO 在整個套件中實現了平均 106 倍的加速（最高達 1103 倍），特別是在複雜的不規則圖形問題上，實現了強勁的 13.6 倍加速，超越了最先進的商用模型。此外，我們的演化方法與最先進的人類專家基準相當，在特定的高度不規則 kernels 上實現了高達 4.1 倍的加速。原始碼和資料集可在 https://github.com/WildAlg/ParEVO 取得。
top5.title: ParEVO: Synthesizing Code for Irregular Data: High-Performance Parallelism through Agentic Evolution
top5.title_zh: ParEVO: 為不規則資料合成程式碼：透過代理演化實現高效能平行處理

links.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models

primary_link.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models

top5.summary: Safety evaluation and red-teaming of large language models remain predominantly text-centric, and existing frameworks lack the infrastructure to systematically test whether alignment generalizes to audio, image, and video inputs. We present MUSE (Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation), an open-source, run-centric platform that integrates automatic cross-modal payload generation, three multi-turn attack algorithms (Crescendo, PAIR, Violent Durian), provider-agnostic model routing, and an LLM judge with a five-level safety taxonomy into a single browser-based system. A dual-metric framework distinguishes hard Attack Success Rate (Compliance only) from soft ASR (including Partial Compliance), capturing partial information leakage that binary metrics miss. To probe whether alignment generalizes across modality boundaries, we introduce Inter-Turn Modality Switching (ITMS), which augments multi-turn attacks with per-turn modality rotation. Experiments across six multimodal LLMs from four providers show that multi-turn strategies can achieve up to 90-100% ASR against models with near-perfect single-turn refusal. ITMS does not uniformly raise final ASR on already-saturated baselines, but accelerates convergence by destabilizing early-turn defenses, and ablation reveals that the direction of modality effects is model-family-specific rather than universal, underscoring the need for provider-aware cross-modal safety testing.
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言模型的安全評估和 red-teaming 仍然主要以文本為中心，現有框架缺乏基礎設施來系統性測試對齊是否能推廣到音訊、圖像和視訊輸入。我們提出了 MUSE (Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation)，一個開源的、以執行為中心的平台，它將自動跨模態 payload 生成、三種多輪攻擊演算法 (Crescendo、PAIR、Violent Durian)、與供應商無關的模型路由，以及一個具有五級安全分類的 LLM 判斷器整合到一個單一的基於瀏覽器的系統中。一個雙重度量框架將硬性 Attack Success Rate (僅 Compliance) 與軟性 ASR (包括 Partial Compliance) 區分開來，捕捉到二元度量會遺漏的部分資訊洩露。為了探究對齊是否能跨模態邊界推廣，我們引入了 Inter-Turn Modality Switching (ITMS)，它透過逐輪模態旋轉來增強多輪攻擊。對來自四個供應商的六個多模態 LLMs 進行的實驗表明，多輪策略可以針對具有近乎完美單輪拒絕的模型實現高達 90-100% 的 ASR。ITMS 並未均勻提高已飽和基準上的最終 ASR，但透過破壞早期防禦的穩定性來加速收斂，並且 ablation 揭示了模態效應的方向是模型家族特異性的而非普遍的，這強調了對供應商感知型跨模態安全測試的需求。
top5.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models
top5.title_zh: MUSE: 一個以執行為中心的平台，用於大型語言模型的多模態統一安全評估

links.title: MemSifter: Offloading LLM Memory Retrieval via Outcome-Driven Proxy Reasoning

primary_link.title: MemSifter: Offloading LLM Memory Retrieval via Outcome-Driven Proxy Reasoning

top5.summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for long-duration tasks, maintaining effective long-term memory has become a critical challenge. Current methods often face a trade-off between cost and accuracy. Simple storage methods often fail to retrieve relevant information, while complex indexing methods (such as memory graphs) require heavy computation and can cause information loss. Furthermore, relying on the working LLM to process all memories is computationally expensive and slow. To address these limitations, we propose MemSifter, a novel framework that offloads the memory retrieval process to a small-scale proxy model. Instead of increasing the burden on the primary working LLM, MemSifter uses a smaller model to reason about the task before retrieving the necessary information. This approach requires no heavy computation during the indexing phase and adds minimal overhead during inference. To optimize the proxy model, we introduce a memory-specific Reinforcement Learning (RL) training paradigm. We design a task-outcome-oriented reward based on the working LLM's actual performance in completing the task. The reward measures the actual contribution of retrieved memories by mutiple interactions with the working LLM, and discriminates retrieved rankings by stepped decreasing contributions. Additionally, we employ training techniques such as Curriculum Learning and Model Merging to improve performance. We evaluated MemSifter on eight LLM memory benchmarks, including Deep Research tasks. The results demonstrate that our method meets or exceeds the performance of existing state-of-the-art approaches in both retrieval accuracy and final task completion. MemSifter offers an efficient and scalable solution for long-term LLM memory. We have open-sourced the model weights, code, and training data to support further research.
top5.summary_zh: 隨著 Large Language Models (LLMs) 越來越多地用於長時間任務，維持有效的長期記憶已成為一個關鍵挑戰。現有方法通常面臨成本和準確性之間的權衡。簡單的儲存方法往往無法檢索相關資訊，而複雜的索引方法（例如 memory graphs）需要大量的計算，並可能導致資訊遺失。此外，依賴工作中的 LLM 處理所有記憶體在計算上昂貴且速度緩慢。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了 MemSifter，一個新穎的框架，它將記憶體檢索過程卸載到一個小型代理模型。MemSifter 不會增加主要工作 LLM 的負擔，而是使用一個較小的模型在檢索必要資訊之前對任務進行推理。這種方法在索引階段不需要大量的計算，並且在推理過程中增加了最小的開銷。為了優化代理模型，我們引入了一種記憶體專用的 Reinforcement Learning (RL) 訓練範式。我們設計了一個以任務結果為導向的獎勵，基於工作 LLM 在完成任務時的實際表現。該獎勵透過與工作 LLM 的多次互動來衡量檢索到的記憶體的實際貢獻，並透過逐步遞減的貢獻來區分檢索到的排名。此外，我們採用了 Curriculum Learning 和 Model Merging 等訓練技術來提高性能。我們在八個 LLM 記憶體基準測試（包括 Deep Research 任務）上評估了 MemSifter。結果表明，我們的方法在檢索準確性和最終任務完成方面達到或超越了現有最先進方法的性能。MemSifter 為 LLM 的長期記憶提供了一個高效且可擴展的解決方案。我們已開源了模型權重、程式碼和訓練資料，以支持進一步的研究。
top5.title: MemSifter: Offloading LLM Memory Retrieval via Outcome-Driven Proxy Reasoning
top5.title_zh: MemSifter: 透過結果導向的代理推理卸載 LLM 記憶體檢索
